transcript: [' Its origin was small, a germ, an insignificant seed, hardly to be thought of as likely to arouse opposition.']
reference: ITS ORIGIN WAS SMALL A GERM AN INSIGNIFICANT SEED HARDLY TO BE THOUGHT OF AS LIKELY TO AROUSE OPPOSITION

transcript: [' Instead of but six regularly affiliated members and at most two score of adherents, the organization numbers today many hundred thousand souls.']
reference: INSTEAD OF BUT SIX REGULARLY AFFILIATED MEMBERS AND AT MOST TWO SCORE OF ADHERENTS THE ORGANIZATION NUMBERS TODAY MANY HUNDRED THOUSAND SOULS

transcript: [' In place of a single hamlet in the smallest corner of which the members could have congregated there now are about seventy stakes of zion and about seven hundred organized wards, each ward and stake with its full complement of officers and priesthood organizations.']
reference: IN PLACE OF A SINGLE HAMLET IN THE SMALLEST CORNER OF WHICH THE MEMBERS COULD HAVE CONGREGATED THERE NOW ARE ABOUT SEVENTY STAKES OF ZION AND ABOUT SEVEN HUNDRED ORGANIZED WARDS EACH WARD AND STAKE WITH ITS FULL COMPLEMENT OF OFFICERS AND PRIESTHOOD ORGANIZATIONS

transcript: [' The practice of gathering its proselytes into one place prevents the building up and strengthening of foreign branches, and inasmuch as extensive and strong organizations are seldom met with abroad, very erroneous ideas exist concerning the strength of the church.']
reference: THE PRACTISE OF GATHERING ITS PROSELYTES INTO ONE PLACE PREVENTS THE BUILDING UP AND STRENGTHENING OF FOREIGN BRANCHES AND INASMUCH AS EXTENSIVE AND STRONG ORGANIZATIONS ARE SELDOM MET WITH ABROAD VERY ERRONEOUS IDEAS EXIST CONCERNING THE STRENGTH OF THE CHURCH

transcript: [' Nevertheless the mustard seed, among the smallest of all seeds, has attained the proportions of a tree and the birds of the air are nesting in its branches. The acorn is now an oak offering protection and the sweets of satisfaction to every earnest pilgrim journeying its way for truth.']
reference: NEVERTHELESS THE MUSTARD SEED AMONG THE SMALLEST OF ALL SEEDS HAS ATTAINED THE PROPORTIONS OF A TREE AND THE BIRDS OF THE AIR ARE NESTING IN ITS BRANCHES THE ACORN IS NOW AN OAK OFFERING PROTECTION AND THE SWEETS OF SATISFACTION TO EVERY EARNEST PILGRIM JOURNEYING ITS WAY FOR TRUTH

transcript: [' Their eyes were from the first turned in anticipation toward the evening sun, not merely that the work of proselyting should be carried on in the west, but that the headquarters of the church should be there established.']
reference: THEIR EYES WERE FROM THE FIRST TURNED IN ANTICIPATION TOWARD THE EVENING SUN NOT MERELY THAT THE WORK OF PROSELYTING SHOULD BE CARRIED ON IN THE WEST BUT THAT THE HEADQUARTERS OF THE CHURCH SHOULD BE THERE ESTABLISHED

transcript: [' The Book of Mormon had taught the people the true origin and destiny of the American Indians and toward this dark skinned remnant of a once mighty people the missionaries of Mormonism early turned their eyes and with their eyes went their hearts and their hopes.']
reference: THE BOOK OF MORMON HAD TAUGHT THE PEOPLE THE TRUE ORIGIN AND DESTINY OF THE AMERICAN INDIANS AND TOWARD THIS DARK SKINNED REMNANT OF A ONCE MIGHTY PEOPLE THE MISSIONARIES OF MORMONISM EARLY TURNED THEIR EYES AND WITH THEIR EYES WENT THEIR HEARTS AND THEIR HOPES

transcript: [' It is notable that the Indian tribes have generally regarded the religion of the latter day saints with favor, seeing in the Book of Mormon striking agreement with their own traditions.']
reference: IT IS NOTABLE THAT THE INDIAN TRIBES HAVE GENERALLY REGARDED THE RELIGION OF THE LATTER DAY SAINTS WITH FAVOR SEEING IN THE BOOK OF MORMON STRIKING AGREEMENT WITH THEIR OWN TRADITIONS

transcript: [' The first well established seat of the church was in the pretty little town of Kirtland, Ohio, almost within sight of lake Erie, and here soon rose the first temple of modern times.']
reference: THE FIRST WELL ESTABLISHED SEAT OF THE CHURCH WAS IN THE PRETTY LITTLE TOWN OF KIRTLAND OHIO ALMOST WITHIN SIGHT OF LAKE ERIE AND HERE SOON ROSE THE FIRST TEMPLE OF MODERN TIMES

transcript: [' To the fervent Latter day Saint, a temple is not simply a church building, a house for religious assembly.']
reference: TO THE FERVENT LATTER DAY SAINT A TEMPLE IS NOT SIMPLY A CHURCH BUILDING A HOUSE FOR RELIGIOUS ASSEMBLY

transcript: [' soon thousands of converts had rented or purchased homes in Missouri, independence Jackson County being their center. But from the first they were unpopular among the Missourians.']
reference: SOON THOUSANDS OF CONVERTS HAD RENTED OR PURCHASED HOMES IN MISSOURI INDEPENDENCE JACKSON COUNTY BEING THEIR CENTER BUT FROM THE FIRST THEY WERE UNPOPULAR AMONG THE MISSOURIANS

transcript: [' Lieutenant Governor Littleburn w Boggs afterward governor was a pronounced Mormon hater and throughout the period of the troubles he manifested sympathy with the persecutors.']
reference: THE LIEUTENANT GOVERNOR LILBURN W BOGGS AFTERWARD GOVERNOR WAS A PRONOUNCED MORMON HATER AND THROUGHOUT THE PERIOD OF THE TROUBLES HE MANIFESTED SYMPATHY WITH THE PERSECUTORS

transcript: [' Their sufferings have never yet been fitly chronicled by human scribe.']
reference: THEIR SUFFERINGS HAVE NEVER YET BEEN FITLY CHRONICLED BY HUMAN SCRIBE

transcript: [' Making their way across the river, most of the refugees found shelter among the more hospitable people of clay county and afterward established themselves in caldwell county, therein founding the city of far west.']
reference: MAKING THEIR WAY ACROSS THE RIVER MOST OF THE REFUGEES FOUND SHELTER AMONG THE MORE HOSPITABLE PEOPLE OF CLAY COUNTY AND AFTERWARD ESTABLISHED THEMSELVES IN CALDWELL COUNTY THEREIN FOUNDING THE CITY OF FAR WEST

transcript: [' A small settlement had been founded by Mormon families on Shoal Creek and here on the thirtieth of October eighteen thirty eight a company of two hundred and forty fell upon the hapless settlers and butchered a score.']
reference: A SMALL SETTLEMENT HAD BEEN FOUNDED BY MORMON FAMILIES ON SHOAL CREEK AND HERE ON THE THIRTIETH OF OCTOBER EIGHTEEN THIRTY EIGHT A COMPANY OF TWO HUNDRED AND FORTY FELL UPON THE HAPLESS SETTLERS AND BUTCHERED A SCORE

transcript: [' Be it said to the honor of some of the officers intrusted with the terrible commission that when they learned its true significance they resigned their authority rather than have anything to do with what they designated a cold blooded butchery.']
reference: BE IT SAID TO THE HONOR OF SOME OF THE OFFICERS ENTRUSTED WITH THE TERRIBLE COMMISSION THAT WHEN THEY LEARNED ITS TRUE SIGNIFICANCE THEY RESIGNED THEIR AUTHORITY RATHER THAN HAVE ANYTHING TO DO WITH WHAT THEY DESIGNATED A COLD BLOODED BUTCHERY

transcript: [' Oh what a record to read what a picture to gaze upon how awful the fact']
reference: OH WHAT A RECORD TO READ WHAT A PICTURE TO GAZE UPON HOW AWFUL THE FACT

transcript: [' American schoolboys read with emotions of horror of the albigenses driven beaten and killed with a papal legate directing the butchery and of the vaudois hunted and hounded like beasts as the effect of a royal decree and they yet shall read in the history of their own country of scenes as terrible as these in the exhibition of injustice and inhuman hate.']
reference: AMERICAN SCHOOL BOYS READ WITH EMOTIONS OF HORROR OF THE ALBIGENSES DRIVEN BEATEN AND KILLED WITH A PAPAL LEGATE DIRECTING THE BUTCHERY AND OF THE VAUDOIS HUNTED AND HOUNDED LIKE BEASTS AS THE EFFECT OF A ROYAL DECREE AND THEY YET SHALL READ IN THE HISTORY OF THEIR OWN COUNTRY OF SCENES AS TERRIBLE AS THESE IN THE EXHIBITION OF INJUSTICE AND INHUMAN HATE

transcript: [' Who began the quarrel? Was it the mormons?']
reference: WHO BEGAN THE QUARREL WAS IT THE MORMONS

transcript: [' As a sample of the press comments against the brutality of the Missourians, I quote a paragraph from the Quincy Argus march sixteenth eighteen thirty nine.']
reference: AS A SAMPLE OF THE PRESS COMMENTS AGAINST THE BRUTALITY OF THE MISSOURIANS I QUOTE A PARAGRAPH FROM THE QUINCY ARGUS MARCH SIXTEENTH EIGHTEEN THIRTY NINE

transcript: [' It will be observed that an organized mob aided by many of the civil and military officers of Missouri with Governor Boggs at their head have been the prominent actors in this business incited too it appears against the mormons by political hatred and by the additional motives of plunder and revenge.']
reference: IT WILL BE OBSERVED THAT AN ORGANIZED MOB AIDED BY MANY OF THE CIVIL AND MILITARY OFFICERS OF MISSOURI WITH GOVERNOR BOGGS AT THEIR HEAD HAVE BEEN THE PROMINENT ACTORS IN THIS BUSINESS INCITED TOO IT APPEARS AGAINST THE MORMONS BY POLITICAL HATRED AND BY THE ADDITIONAL MOTIVES OF PLUNDER AND REVENGE

transcript: [' The army found the people in poverty and left them in comparative wealth.']
reference: THE ARMY FOUND THE PEOPLE IN POVERTY AND LEFT THEM IN COMPARATIVE WEALTH

transcript: [' But a word further concerning the expedition in general.']
reference: BUT A WORD FURTHER CONCERNING THE EXPEDITION IN GENERAL

transcript: [" It was through Floyd's advice that buchanan ordered a military expedition to Utah ostensibly to install certain federal officials and to repress an alleged infantile rebellion which in fact had never come into existence but in reality to further the interests of the secessionists."]
reference: IT WAS THROUGH FLOYD'S ADVICE THAT BUCHANAN ORDERED THE MILITARY EXPEDITION TO UTAH OSTENSIBLY TO INSTALL CERTAIN FEDERAL OFFICIALS AND TO REPRESS AN ALLEGED INFANTILE REBELLION WHICH IN FACT HAD NEVER COME INTO EXISTENCE BUT IN REALITY TO FURTHER THE INTERESTS OF THE SECESSIONISTS

transcript: [' Moreover, had the people been inclined to rebellion, what greater opportunity could they have wished?']
reference: MOREOVER HAD THE PEOPLE BEEN INCLINED TO REBELLION WHAT GREATER OPPORTUNITY COULD THEY HAVE WISHED

transcript: [' Already a north and a south were talked of why not set up also a west?']
reference: ALREADY A NORTH AND A SOUTH WERE TALKED OF WHY NOT SET UP ALSO A WEST

transcript: [" They knew no north no south no east no west they stood positively by the constitution and would have nothing to do in the bloody strife between brothers unless indeed they were summoned by the authority to which they had already once loyally responded to furnish men and arms for their country's need."]
reference: THEY KNEW NO NORTH NO SOUTH NO EAST NO WEST THEY STOOD POSITIVELY BY THE CONSTITUTION AND WOULD HAVE NOTHING TO DO IN THE BLOODY STRIFE BETWEEN BROTHERS UNLESS INDEED THEY WERE SUMMONED BY THE AUTHORITY TO WHICH THEY HAD ALREADY ONCE LOYALLY RESPONDED TO FURNISH MEN AND ARMS FOR THEIR COUNTRY'S NEED

transcript: [' What the latter day saints call celestial marriage is characteristic of the church and is in very general practice but of celestial marriage plurality of wives was an incident never an essential.']
reference: WHAT THE LATTER DAY SAINTS CALL CELESTIAL MARRIAGE IS CHARACTERISTIC OF THE CHURCH AND IS IN VERY GENERAL PRACTISE BUT OF CELESTIAL MARRIAGE PLURALITY OF WIVES WAS AN INCIDENT NEVER AN ESSENTIAL

transcript: [' We believe in a literal resurrection and an actual hereafter in which future state shall be recognized every sanctified and authorized relationship existing here on earth of parent and child brother and sister husband and wife.']
reference: WE BELIEVE IN A LITERAL RESURRECTION AND AN ACTUAL HEREAFTER IN WHICH FUTURE STATE SHALL BE RECOGNIZED EVERY SANCTIFIED AND AUTHORIZED RELATIONSHIP EXISTING HERE ON EARTH OF PARENT AND CHILD BROTHER AND SISTER HUSBAND AND WIFE

transcript: [' It has been my privilege to tread the soil of many lands to observe the customs and study the habits of more nations than one and I have yet to find the place and meet the people where and with whom the purity of man and woman is held more precious than among the maligned mormons in the mountain valleys of the west.']
reference: IT HAS BEEN MY PRIVILEGE TO TREAD THE SOIL OF MANY LANDS TO OBSERVE THE CUSTOMS AND STUDY THE HABITS OF MORE NATIONS THAN ONE AND I HAVE YET TO FIND THE PLACE AND MEET THE PEOPLE WHERE AND WITH WHOM THE PURITY OF MAN AND WOMAN IS HELD MORE PRECIOUS THAN AMONG THE MALIGNED MORMONS IN THE MOUNTAIN VALLEYS OF THE WEST

transcript: [' At the inception of plural marriage among the latter day saints, there was no law national or state against its practice.']
reference: AT THE INCEPTION OF PLURAL MARRIAGE AMONG THE LATTER DAY SAINTS THERE WAS NO LAW NATIONAL OR STATE AGAINST ITS PRACTISE

transcript: [' In eighteen sixty two a law was enacted with the purpose of suppressing plural marriage and as had been predicted in the national senate prior to its passage it lay for many years a dead letter.']
reference: IN EIGHTEEN SIXTY TWO A LAW WAS ENACTED WITH THE PURPOSE OF SUPPRESSING PLURAL MARRIAGE AND AS HAD BEEN PREDICTED IN THE NATIONAL SENATE PRIOR TO ITS PASSAGE IT LAY FOR MANY YEARS A DEAD LETTER

transcript: [' Federal judges and United States attorneys in Utah who were not mormons nor lovers of mormonism refused to entertain complaints or prosecute cases under the law because of its manifest injustice and inadequacy.']
reference: FEDERAL JUDGES AND UNITED STATES ATTORNEYS IN UTAH WHO WERE NOT MORMONS NOR LOVERS OF MORMONISM REFUSED TO ENTERTAIN COMPLAINTS OR PROSECUTE CASES UNDER THE LAW BECAUSE OF ITS MANIFEST INJUSTICE AND INADEQUACY

transcript: [" This meant that for an alleged misdemeanor, for which Congress prescribed a maximum penalty of six months imprisonment and a fine of three hundred dollars, a man might be imprisoned for life, ay, for many terms of a man's natural life, did the court's power to enforce its sentences extend so far, and might be fined millions of dollars."]
reference: THIS MEANT THAT FOR AN ALLEGED MISDEMEANOR FOR WHICH CONGRESS PRESCRIBED A MAXIMUM PENALTY OF SIX MONTHS IMPRISONMENT AND A FINE OF THREE HUNDRED DOLLARS A MAN MIGHT BE IMPRISONED FOR LIFE AYE FOR MANY TERMS OF A MAN'S NATURAL LIFE DID THE COURT'S POWER TO ENFORCE ITS SENTENCES EXTEND SO FAR AND MIGHT BE FINED MILLIONS OF DOLLARS

transcript: [' Before this travesty on the administration of law could be brought before the court of last resort and there met with the reversal and rebuke it deserved, men were imprisoned under sentence of many years duration.']
reference: BEFORE THIS TRAVESTY ON THE ADMINISTRATION OF LAW COULD BE BROUGHT BEFORE THE COURT OF LAST RESORT AND THERE MEET WITH THE REVERSAL AND REBUKE IT DESERVED MEN WERE IMPRISONED UNDER SENTENCES OF MANY YEARS DURATION

transcript: [' The people contested these measures one by one in the courts presenting in case after case the different phases of the subject and urging the unconstitutionality of the measure.']
reference: THE PEOPLE CONTESTED THESE MEASURES ONE BY ONE IN THE COURTS PRESENTING IN CASE AFTER CASE THE DIFFERENT PHASES OF THE SUBJECT AND URGING THE UNCONSTITUTIONALITY OF THE MEASURE

transcript: [" Then the church was disincorporated and its property both real and personal confiscated and escheated to the government of the United States and although the personal property was soon restored real estate of great value long lay in the hands of the court's receiver and the Mormon church had to pay the national government high rental on its own property."]
reference: THEN THE CHURCH WAS DISINCORPORATED AND ITS PROPERTY BOTH REAL AND PERSONAL CONFISCATED AND ESCHEATED TO THE GOVERNMENT OF THE UNITED STATES AND ALTHOUGH THE PERSONAL PROPERTY WAS SOON RESTORED REAL ESTATE OF GREAT VALUE LONG LAY IN THE HANDS OF THE COURT'S RECEIVER AND THE MORMON CHURCH HAD TO PAY THE NATIONAL GOVERNMENT HIGH RENTAL ON ITS OWN PROPERTY

transcript: [' And so the story of mormonism runs on its finale has not yet been written the current press presents continuously new stages of its progress new developments of its plan.']
reference: AND SO THE STORY OF MORMONISM RUNS ON ITS FINALE HAS NOT YET BEEN WRITTEN THE CURRENT PRESS PRESENTS CONTINUOUSLY NEW STAGES OF ITS PROGRESS NEW DEVELOPMENTS OF ITS PLAN

reference: MAINHALL LIKED ALEXANDER BECAUSE HE WAS AN ENGINEER

reference: HE HAD PRECONCEIVED IDEAS ABOUT EVERYTHING AND HIS IDEA ABOUT AMERICANS WAS THAT THEY SHOULD BE ENGINEERS OR MECHANICS

reference: IT'S TREMENDOUSLY WELL PUT ON TOO

reference: IT'S BEEN ON ONLY TWO WEEKS AND I'VE BEEN HALF A DOZEN TIMES ALREADY

reference: DO YOU KNOW ALEXANDER MAINHALL LOOKED WITH PERPLEXITY UP INTO THE TOP OF THE HANSOM AND RUBBED HIS PINK CHEEK WITH HIS GLOVED FINGER DO YOU KNOW I SOMETIMES THINK OF TAKING TO CRITICISM SERIOUSLY MYSELF

reference: SHE SAVES HER HAND TOO SHE'S AT HER BEST IN THE SECOND ACT

reference: HE'S BEEN WANTING TO MARRY HILDA THESE THREE YEARS AND MORE

reference: SHE DOESN'T TAKE UP WITH ANYBODY YOU KNOW

reference: IRENE BURGOYNE ONE OF HER FAMILY TOLD ME IN CONFIDENCE THAT THERE WAS A ROMANCE SOMEWHERE BACK IN THE BEGINNING

reference: MAINHALL VOUCHED FOR HER CONSTANCY WITH A LOFTINESS THAT MADE ALEXANDER SMILE EVEN WHILE A KIND OF RAPID EXCITEMENT WAS TINGLING THROUGH HIM

reference: HE'S ANOTHER WHO'S AWFULLY KEEN ABOUT HER LET ME INTRODUCE YOU

reference: SIR HARRY TOWNE MISTER BARTLEY ALEXANDER THE AMERICAN ENGINEER

reference: I SAY SIR HARRY THE LITTLE GIRL'S GOING FAMOUSLY TO NIGHT ISN'T SHE

reference: DO YOU KNOW I THOUGHT THE DANCE A BIT CONSCIOUS TO NIGHT FOR THE FIRST TIME

reference: WESTMERE AND I WERE BACK AFTER THE FIRST ACT AND WE THOUGHT SHE SEEMED QUITE UNCERTAIN OF HERSELF

reference: A LITTLE ATTACK OF NERVES POSSIBLY

reference: HE WAS BEGINNING TO FEEL A KEEN INTEREST IN THE SLENDER BAREFOOT DONKEY GIRL WHO SLIPPED IN AND OUT OF THE PLAY SINGING LIKE SOME ONE WINDING THROUGH A HILLY FIELD

reference: ONE NIGHT WHEN HE AND WINIFRED WERE SITTING TOGETHER ON THE BRIDGE HE TOLD HER THAT THINGS HAD HAPPENED WHILE HE WAS STUDYING ABROAD THAT HE WAS SORRY FOR ONE THING IN PARTICULAR AND HE ASKED HER WHETHER SHE THOUGHT SHE OUGHT TO KNOW ABOUT THEM

reference: SHE CONSIDERED A MOMENT AND THEN SAID NO I THINK NOT THOUGH I AM GLAD YOU ASK ME

reference: AFTER THAT IT WAS EASY TO FORGET ACTUALLY TO FORGET

reference: OF COURSE HE REFLECTED SHE ALWAYS HAD THAT COMBINATION OF SOMETHING HOMELY AND SENSIBLE AND SOMETHING UTTERLY WILD AND DAFT

reference: SHE MUST CARE ABOUT THE THEATRE A GREAT DEAL MORE THAN SHE USED TO

reference: I SHOULDN'T WONDER IF SHE COULD LAUGH ABOUT IT WITH ME NOW

reference: THEY ASKED HIM TO COME TO SEE THEM IN CHELSEA AND THEY SPOKE VERY TENDERLY OF HILDA

reference: LAMB WOULDN'T CARE A GREAT DEAL ABOUT MANY OF THEM I FANCY

reference: THEY ARE ALL SKETCHES MADE ABOUT THE VILLA D'ESTE YOU SEE

reference: THOSE FELLOWS ARE ALL VERY LOYAL EVEN MAINHALL

reference: I'VE MANAGED TO SAVE SOMETHING EVERY YEAR AND THAT WITH HELPING MY THREE SISTERS NOW AND THEN AND TIDING POOR COUSIN MIKE OVER BAD SEASONS

reference: THERE IS NOTHING ELSE THAT LOOKS SO JOLLY

reference: THANK YOU BUT I DON'T LIKE IT SO WELL AS THIS

reference: HAVE YOU BEEN IN PARIS MUCH THESE LATE YEARS

reference: DON'T I THOUGH I'M SO SORRY TO HEAR IT HOW DID HER SON TURN OUT

reference: HER HAIR IS STILL LIKE FLAX AND HER BLUE EYES ARE JUST LIKE A BABY'S AND SHE HAS THE SAME THREE FRECKLES ON HER LITTLE NOSE AND TALKS ABOUT GOING BACK TO HER BAINS DE MER

reference: HOW JOLLY IT WAS BEING YOUNG HILDA

reference: I THINK WE DID SHE ANSWERED DEMURELY

reference: WHAT SHE WANTED FROM US WAS NEITHER OUR FLOWERS NOR OUR FRANCS BUT JUST OUR YOUTH

reference: THEY WERE BOTH REMEMBERING WHAT THE WOMAN HAD SAID WHEN SHE TOOK THE MONEY GOD GIVE YOU A HAPPY LOVE

reference: THE STRANGE WOMAN AND HER PASSIONATE SENTENCE THAT RANG OUT SO SHARPLY HAD FRIGHTENED THEM BOTH

reference: HAVE I TOLD YOU ABOUT MY NEW PLAY

reference: WHEN SHE FINISHED ALEXANDER SHOOK HIMSELF OUT OF A REVERIE

reference: NONSENSE OF COURSE I CAN'T REALLY SING EXCEPT THE WAY MY MOTHER AND GRANDMOTHER DID BEFORE ME

reference: ALEXANDER WENT OVER AND OPENED THE WINDOW FOR HER

reference: FOR A LONG TIME NEITHER HILDA NOR BARTLEY SPOKE

reference: HE FELT A TREMOR RUN THROUGH THE SLENDER YELLOW FIGURE IN FRONT OF HIM

reference: ALEXANDER UNCLENCHED THE TWO HANDS AT HIS SIDES

reference: THE STOP AT QUEENSTOWN THE TEDIOUS PASSAGE UP THE MERSEY WERE THINGS THAT HE NOTED DIMLY THROUGH HIS GROWING IMPATIENCE

reference: THE ROOM WAS EMPTY WHEN HE ENTERED

reference: I GOT IN ABOUT TEN MINUTES AGO

reference: IT WAS MYSELF I WAS DEFYING HILDA

reference: ALEXANDER ROSE AND SHOOK HIMSELF ANGRILY YES I KNOW I'M COWARDLY

reference: HE TOOK HER ROUGHLY IN HIS ARMS DO YOU KNOW WHAT I MEAN

reference: I WILL ASK THE LEAST IMAGINABLE BUT I MUST HAVE SOMETHING

reference: THE SIGHT OF YOU BARTLEY TO SEE YOU LIVING AND HAPPY AND SUCCESSFUL CAN I NEVER MAKE YOU UNDERSTAND WHAT THAT MEANS TO ME

reference: CHAPTER ONE ORIGIN

reference: IT ENGENDERS A WHOLE WORLD LA PEGRE FOR WHICH READ THEFT AND A HELL LA PEGRENNE FOR WHICH READ HUNGER

reference: WHY SHOULD ONE HALT ON THE WAY

transcript: [' Nothing is more lugubrious than the contemplation thus in its nudity in the broad light of thought of the horrible swarming of slang.']
reference: NOTHING IS MORE LUGUBRIOUS THAN THE CONTEMPLATION THUS IN ITS NUDITY IN THE BROAD LIGHT OF THOUGHT OF THE HORRIBLE SWARMING OF SLANG

transcript: [' Now, when has horror ever excluded study?']
reference: NOW WHEN HAS HORROR EVER EXCLUDED STUDY

transcript: [' Since when has malady banished medicine?']
reference: SINCE WHEN HAS MALADY BANISHED MEDICINE

transcript: [' Can one imagine a naturalist refusing to study the viper, the bat, the scorpion, the centipede, the tarantula, and one who would cast them back into their darkness saying, Oh, how ugly that is!']
reference: CAN ONE IMAGINE A NATURALIST REFUSING TO STUDY THE VIPER THE BAT THE SCORPION THE CENTIPEDE THE TARANTULA AND ONE WHO WOULD CAST THEM BACK INTO THEIR DARKNESS SAYING OH HOW UGLY THAT IS

transcript: [' He would be like a philologist refusing to examine a fact in language, a philosopher hesitating to scrutinize a fact in humanity.']
reference: HE WOULD BE LIKE A PHILOLOGIST REFUSING TO EXAMINE A FACT IN LANGUAGE A PHILOSOPHER HESITATING TO SCRUTINIZE A FACT IN HUMANITY

transcript: [' What is slang, properly speaking?']
reference: WHAT IS SLANG PROPERLY SPEAKING

transcript: [' It is the language of wretchedness.']
reference: IT IS THE LANGUAGE OF WRETCHEDNESS

transcript: [' We may be stopped, the fact may be put to us in general terms, which is one way of attenuating it, we may be told that all trades professions, it may be added, all the accidents of the social hierarchy and all forms of intelligence have their own slang.']
reference: WE MAY BE STOPPED THE FACT MAY BE PUT TO US IN GENERAL TERMS WHICH IS ONE WAY OF ATTENUATING IT WE MAY BE TOLD THAT ALL TRADES PROFESSIONS IT MAY BE ADDED ALL THE ACCIDENTS OF THE SOCIAL HIERARCHY AND ALL FORMS OF INTELLIGENCE HAVE THEIR OWN SLANG

transcript: [' The painter who says my grinder, the notary who says my skip the gutter, the hairdresser who says my mealy back, the cobbler who says my cub talks slang.']
reference: THE PAINTER WHO SAYS MY GRINDER THE NOTARY WHO SAYS MY SKIP THE GUTTER THE HAIRDRESSER WHO SAYS MY MEALYBACK THE COBBLER WHO SAYS MY CUB TALKS SLANG

transcript: [' There is the slang of the affected lady as well as of the preciuses.']
reference: THERE IS THE SLANG OF THE AFFECTED LADY AS WELL AS OF THE PRECIEUSES

transcript: [' The sugar manufacturer who says loaf clarified lumps bastard common burnt. This honest manufacturer talks slang.']
reference: THE SUGAR MANUFACTURER WHO SAYS LOAF CLARIFIED LUMPS BASTARD COMMON BURNT THIS HONEST MANUFACTURER TALKS SLANG

transcript: [' Algebra, medicine, botany have each their slang.']
reference: ALGEBRA MEDICINE BOTANY HAVE EACH THEIR SLANG

transcript: [' To meet the needs of this conflict, wretchedness has invented a language of combat, which is slang.']
reference: TO MEET THE NEEDS OF THIS CONFLICT WRETCHEDNESS HAS INVENTED A LANGUAGE OF COMBAT WHICH IS SLANG

transcript: [' To keep afloat and to rescue from oblivion to hold above the gulf were it but a fragment of some language which man has spoken and which would otherwise be lost that is to say one of the elements good or bad of which civilization is composed or by which it is complicated to extend the records of social observation is to serve civilization itself.']
reference: TO KEEP AFLOAT AND TO RESCUE FROM OBLIVION TO HOLD ABOVE THE GULF WERE IT BUT A FRAGMENT OF SOME LANGUAGE WHICH MAN HAS SPOKEN AND WHICH WOULD OTHERWISE BE LOST THAT IS TO SAY ONE OF THE ELEMENTS GOOD OR BAD OF WHICH CIVILIZATION IS COMPOSED OR BY WHICH IT IS COMPLICATED TO EXTEND THE RECORDS OF SOCIAL OBSERVATION IS TO SERVE CIVILIZATION ITSELF

transcript: [' Phoenician very good']
reference: PHOENICIAN VERY GOOD

transcript: [' Even dialect, let that pass.']
reference: EVEN DIALECT LET THAT PASS

transcript: [' To this we reply in one word only.']
reference: TO THIS WE REPLY IN ONE WORD ONLY

transcript: [' Assuredly if the tongue which a nation or a province has spoken is worthy of interest, the language which has been spoken by a misery is still more worthy of attention and study.']
reference: ASSUREDLY IF THE TONGUE WHICH A NATION OR A PROVINCE HAS SPOKEN IS WORTHY OF INTEREST THE LANGUAGE WHICH HAS BEEN SPOKEN BY A MISERY IS STILL MORE WORTHY OF ATTENTION AND STUDY

transcript: [' And then we insist upon it, the study of social deformities and infirmities and the task of pointing them out with a view to remedy is not a business in which choice is permitted.']
reference: AND THEN WE INSIST UPON IT THE STUDY OF SOCIAL DEFORMITIES AND INFIRMITIES AND THE TASK OF POINTING THEM OUT WITH A VIEW TO REMEDY IS NOT A BUSINESS IN WHICH CHOICE IS PERMITTED

transcript: [' He must descend with his heart full of charity and severity at the same time, as a brother and as a judge, to those impenetrable casemates where crawl pell mell those who bleed and those who deal the blow, those who weep and those who curse, those who fast and those who devour, those who endure evil and those who inflict it.']
reference: HE MUST DESCEND WITH HIS HEART FULL OF CHARITY AND SEVERITY AT THE SAME TIME AS A BROTHER AND AS A JUDGE TO THOSE IMPENETRABLE CASEMATES WHERE CRAWL PELL MELL THOSE WHO BLEED AND THOSE WHO DEAL THE BLOW THOSE WHO WEEP AND THOSE WHO CURSE THOSE WHO FAST AND THOSE WHO DEVOUR THOSE WHO ENDURE EVIL AND THOSE WHO INFLICT IT

transcript: [' Do we really know the mountain well when we are not acquainted with the cavern?']
reference: DO WE REALLY KNOW THE MOUNTAIN WELL WHEN WE ARE NOT ACQUAINTED WITH THE CAVERN

transcript: [' They constitute two different orders of facts which correspond to each other, which are always interlaced, and which often bring forth results.']
reference: THEY CONSTITUTE TWO DIFFERENT ORDERS OF FACTS WHICH CORRESPOND TO EACH OTHER WHICH ARE ALWAYS INTERLACED AND WHICH OFTEN BRING FORTH RESULTS

transcript: [' True history being a mixture of all things, the true historian mingles in everything.']
reference: TRUE HISTORY BEING A MIXTURE OF ALL THINGS THE TRUE HISTORIAN MINGLES IN EVERYTHING

transcript: [' Facts form one of these and ideas the other.']
reference: FACTS FORM ONE OF THESE AND IDEAS THE OTHER

reference: THERE IT CLOTHES ITSELF IN WORD MASKS IN METAPHOR RAGS

transcript: [' In this guise it becomes horrible.']
reference: IN THIS GUISE IT BECOMES HORRIBLE

transcript: [' One perceives without understanding it a hideous murmur sounding almost like human accents but more nearly resembling a howl than an articulate word.']
reference: ONE PERCEIVES WITHOUT UNDERSTANDING IT A HIDEOUS MURMUR SOUNDING ALMOST LIKE HUMAN ACCENTS BUT MORE NEARLY RESEMBLING A HOWL THAN AN ARTICULATE WORD

reference: ONE THINKS ONE HEARS HYDRAS TALKING

reference: IT IS UNINTELLIGIBLE IN THE DARK

reference: IT IS BLACK IN MISFORTUNE IT IS BLACKER STILL IN CRIME THESE TWO BLACKNESSES AMALGAMATED COMPOSE SLANG

reference: THE EARTH IS NOT DEVOID OF RESEMBLANCE TO A JAIL

transcript: [' Look closely at life.']
reference: LOOK CLOSELY AT LIFE

transcript: [' It is so made that everywhere we feel the sense of punishment.']
reference: IT IS SO MADE THAT EVERYWHERE WE FEEL THE SENSE OF PUNISHMENT

transcript: [' Each day has its own great grief or its little care.']
reference: EACH DAY HAS ITS OWN GREAT GRIEF OR ITS LITTLE CARE

transcript: [' Yesterday you were trembling for a health that is dear to you. Today you fear for your own. Tomorrow it will be anxiety about money. The day after tomorrow the diatribe of a slanderer. The day after that the misfortune of some friend. Then the prevailing weather. Then something that has been broken or lost. Then a pleasure with which your conscience and your vertebral column reproach you. Again, the course of public affairs.']
reference: YESTERDAY YOU WERE TREMBLING FOR A HEALTH THAT IS DEAR TO YOU TO DAY YOU FEAR FOR YOUR OWN TO MORROW IT WILL BE ANXIETY ABOUT MONEY THE DAY AFTER TO MORROW THE DIATRIBE OF A SLANDERER THE DAY AFTER THAT THE MISFORTUNE OF SOME FRIEND THEN THE PREVAILING WEATHER THEN SOMETHING THAT HAS BEEN BROKEN OR LOST THEN A PLEASURE WITH WHICH YOUR CONSCIENCE AND YOUR VERTEBRAL COLUMN REPROACH YOU AGAIN THE COURSE OF PUBLIC AFFAIRS

transcript: [' This without reckoning in the pains of the heart, and so it goes on.']
reference: THIS WITHOUT RECKONING IN THE PAINS OF THE HEART AND SO IT GOES ON

transcript: [' There is hardly one day out of a hundred which is wholly joyous and sunny.']
reference: THERE IS HARDLY ONE DAY OUT OF A HUNDRED WHICH IS WHOLLY JOYOUS AND SUNNY

reference: AND YOU BELONG TO THAT SMALL CLASS WHO ARE HAPPY

transcript: [" In this world's evidently the vestibule of another, there are no fortunate."]
reference: IN THIS WORLD EVIDENTLY THE VESTIBULE OF ANOTHER THERE ARE NO FORTUNATE

transcript: [' The real human division is this, the luminous and the shady.']
reference: THE REAL HUMAN DIVISION IS THIS THE LUMINOUS AND THE SHADY

reference: TO DIMINISH THE NUMBER OF THE SHADY TO AUGMENT THE NUMBER OF THE LUMINOUS THAT IS THE OBJECT

transcript: [' That is why we cry education, science.']
reference: THAT IS WHY WE CRY EDUCATION SCIENCE

transcript: [' To teach reading means to light the fire. Every syllable spelled out sparkles.']
reference: TO TEACH READING MEANS TO LIGHT THE FIRE EVERY SYLLABLE SPELLED OUT SPARKLES

transcript: [' However, he who says light does not necessarily say joy.']
reference: HOWEVER HE WHO SAYS LIGHT DOES NOT NECESSARILY SAY JOY

reference: PEOPLE SUFFER IN THE LIGHT EXCESS BURNS

transcript: [' The flame is the enemy of the wing.']
reference: THE FLAME IS THE ENEMY OF THE WING

transcript: [' To burn without ceasing to fly. Therein lies the marvel of genius.']
reference: TO BURN WITHOUT CEASING TO FLY THEREIN LIES THE MARVEL OF GENIUS

transcript: [" You'll never dig it out of the Astor library."]
reference: YOU'LL NEVER DIG IT OUT OF THE ASTOR LIBRARY

transcript: [' To the young American here or elsewhere the paths to fortune are innumerable and all open there is invitation in the air and success in all his wide horizon.']
reference: TO THE YOUNG AMERICAN HERE OR ELSEWHERE THE PATHS TO FORTUNE ARE INNUMERABLE AND ALL OPEN THERE IS INVITATION IN THE AIR AND SUCCESS IN ALL HIS WIDE HORIZON

reference: HE HAS NO TRADITIONS TO BIND HIM OR GUIDE HIM AND HIS IMPULSE IS TO BREAK AWAY FROM THE OCCUPATION HIS FATHER HAS FOLLOWED AND MAKE A NEW WAY FOR HIMSELF

reference: THE MODEST FELLOW WOULD HAVE LIKED FAME THRUST UPON HIM FOR SOME WORTHY ACHIEVEMENT IT MIGHT BE FOR A BOOK OR FOR THE SKILLFUL MANAGEMENT OF SOME GREAT NEWSPAPER OR FOR SOME DARING EXPEDITION LIKE THAT OF LIEUTENANT STRAIN OR DOCTOR KANE

reference: HE WAS UNABLE TO DECIDE EXACTLY WHAT IT SHOULD BE

reference: SOMETIMES HE THOUGHT HE WOULD LIKE TO STAND IN A CONSPICUOUS PULPIT AND HUMBLY PREACH THE GOSPEL OF REPENTANCE AND IT EVEN CROSSED HIS MIND THAT IT WOULD BE NOBLE TO GIVE HIMSELF TO A MISSIONARY LIFE TO SOME BENIGHTED REGION WHERE THE DATE PALM GROWS AND THE NIGHTINGALE'S VOICE IS IN TUNE AND THE BUL BUL SINGS ON THE OFF NIGHTS

transcript: [' Law seemed to him well enough as a science, but he never could discover a practical case where it appeared to him worth while to go to law, and all the clients who stopped with this new clerk in the anteroom of the law office where he was writing, Philip invariably advised to settle, no matter how, but settle, greatly to the disgust of his employer, who knew that justice between man and man could only be attained by the recognized processes with the attendant fees.']
reference: LAW SEEMED TO HIM WELL ENOUGH AS A SCIENCE BUT HE NEVER COULD DISCOVER A PRACTICAL CASE WHERE IT APPEARED TO HIM WORTH WHILE TO GO TO LAW AND ALL THE CLIENTS WHO STOPPED WITH THIS NEW CLERK IN THE ANTE ROOM OF THE LAW OFFICE WHERE HE WAS WRITING PHILIP INVARIABLY ADVISED TO SETTLE NO MATTER HOW BUT SETTLE GREATLY TO THE DISGUST OF HIS EMPLOYER WHO KNEW THAT JUSTICE BETWEEN MAN AND MAN COULD ONLY BE ATTAINED BY THE RECOGNIZED PROCESSES WITH THE ATTENDANT FEES

transcript: [' It is such a noble ambition that it is a pity it has usually such a shallow foundation.']
reference: IT IS SUCH A NOBLE AMBITION THAT IT IS A PITY IT HAS USUALLY SUCH A SHALLOW FOUNDATION

reference: HE WANTED TO BEGIN AT THE TOP OF THE LADDER

reference: PHILIP THEREFORE READ DILIGENTLY IN THE ASTOR LIBRARY PLANNED LITERARY WORKS THAT SHOULD COMPEL ATTENTION AND NURSED HIS GENIUS

reference: HE HAD NO FRIEND WISE ENOUGH TO TELL HIM TO STEP INTO THE DORKING CONVENTION THEN IN SESSION MAKE A SKETCH OF THE MEN AND WOMEN ON THE PLATFORM AND TAKE IT TO THE EDITOR OF THE DAILY GRAPEVINE AND SEE WHAT HE COULD GET A LINE FOR IT

reference: O VERY WELL SAID GRINGO TURNING AWAY WITH A SHADE OF CONTEMPT YOU'LL FIND IF YOU ARE GOING INTO LITERATURE AND NEWSPAPER WORK THAT YOU CAN'T AFFORD A CONSCIENCE LIKE THAT

reference: BUT PHILIP DID AFFORD IT AND HE WROTE THANKING HIS FRIENDS AND DECLINING BECAUSE HE SAID THE POLITICAL SCHEME WOULD FAIL AND OUGHT TO FAIL

reference: AND HE WENT BACK TO HIS BOOKS AND TO HIS WAITING FOR AN OPENING LARGE ENOUGH FOR HIS DIGNIFIED ENTRANCE INTO THE LITERARY WORLD

reference: WELL I'M GOING AS AN ENGINEER YOU CAN GO AS ONE

reference: YOU CAN BEGIN BY CARRYING A ROD AND PUTTING DOWN THE FIGURES

reference: NO ITS NOT TOO SOON

reference: I'VE BEEN READY TO GO ANYWHERE FOR SIX MONTHS

reference: THE TWO YOUNG MEN WHO WERE BY THIS TIME FULL OF THE ADVENTURE WENT DOWN TO THE WALL STREET OFFICE OF HENRY'S UNCLE AND HAD A TALK WITH THAT WILY OPERATOR

reference: THE NIGHT WAS SPENT IN PACKING UP AND WRITING LETTERS FOR PHILIP WOULD NOT TAKE SUCH AN IMPORTANT STEP WITHOUT INFORMING HIS FRIENDS

reference: WHY IT'S IN MISSOURI SOMEWHERE ON THE FRONTIER I THINK WE'LL GET A MAP

reference: I WAS AFRAID IT WAS NEARER HOME

reference: HE KNEW HIS UNCLE WOULD BE GLAD TO HEAR THAT HE HAD AT LAST TURNED HIS THOUGHTS TO A PRACTICAL MATTER

reference: HE WELL KNEW THE PERILS OF THE FRONTIER THE SAVAGE STATE OF SOCIETY THE LURKING INDIANS AND THE DANGERS OF FEVER

reference: SHE WAS TIRED OF OTHER THINGS

reference: SHE TRIED THIS MORNING AN AIR OR TWO UPON THE PIANO SANG A SIMPLE SONG IN A SWEET BUT SLIGHTLY METALLIC VOICE AND THEN SEATING HERSELF BY THE OPEN WINDOW READ PHILIP'S LETTER

reference: WELL MOTHER SAID THE YOUNG STUDENT LOOKING UP WITH A SHADE OF IMPATIENCE

reference: I HOPE THEE TOLD THE ELDERS THAT FATHER AND I ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE PIANO AND THAT MUCH AS THEE LOVES MUSIC THEE IS NEVER IN THE ROOM WHEN IT IS PLAYED

reference: I HEARD FATHER TELL COUSIN ABNER THAT HE WAS WHIPPED SO OFTEN FOR WHISTLING WHEN HE WAS A BOY THAT HE WAS DETERMINED TO HAVE WHAT COMPENSATION HE COULD GET NOW

reference: THY WAYS GREATLY TRY ME RUTH AND ALL THY RELATIONS

reference: IS THY FATHER WILLING THEE SHOULD GO AWAY TO A SCHOOL OF THE WORLD'S PEOPLE

reference: I HAVE NOT ASKED HIM RUTH REPLIED WITH A LOOK THAT MIGHT IMPLY THAT SHE WAS ONE OF THOSE DETERMINED LITTLE BODIES WHO FIRST MADE UP HER OWN MIND AND THEN COMPELLED OTHERS TO MAKE UP THEIRS IN ACCORDANCE WITH HERS

reference: MOTHER I'M GOING TO STUDY MEDICINE

reference: MARGARET BOLTON ALMOST LOST FOR A MOMENT HER HABITUAL PLACIDITY

transcript: [' The study medicine.']
reference: THEE STUDY MEDICINE

transcript: [' does thee think thee could stand it six months?']
reference: DOES THEE THINK THEE COULD STAND IT SIX MONTHS

reference: AND BESIDES SUPPOSE THEE DOES LEARN MEDICINE

transcript: [' I will practice it.']
reference: I WILL PRACTICE IT

reference: WHERE THEE AND THY FAMILY ARE KNOWN

reference: IF I CAN GET PATIENTS

reference: RUTH SAT QUITE STILL FOR A TIME WITH FACE INTENT AND FLUSHED IT WAS OUT NOW

reference: THE SIGHT SEERS RETURNED IN HIGH SPIRITS FROM THE CITY

reference: RUTH ASKED THE ENTHUSIASTS IF THEY WOULD LIKE TO LIVE IN SUCH A SOUNDING MAUSOLEUM WITH ITS GREAT HALLS AND ECHOING ROOMS AND NO COMFORTABLE PLACE IN IT FOR THE ACCOMMODATION OF ANY BODY

reference: AND THEN THERE WAS BROAD STREET

reference: THERE CERTAINLY WAS NO END TO IT AND EVEN RUTH WAS PHILADELPHIAN ENOUGH TO BELIEVE THAT A STREET OUGHT NOT TO HAVE ANY END OR ARCHITECTURAL POINT UPON WHICH THE WEARY EYE COULD REST

reference: BUT NEITHER SAINT GIRARD NOR BROAD STREET NEITHER WONDERS OF THE MINT NOR THE GLORIES OF THE HALL WHERE THE GHOSTS OF OUR FATHERS SIT ALWAYS SIGNING THE DECLARATION IMPRESSED THE VISITORS SO MUCH AS THE SPLENDORS OF THE CHESTNUT STREET WINDOWS AND THE BARGAINS ON EIGHTH STREET

reference: IS THEE GOING TO THE YEARLY MEETING RUTH ASKED ONE OF THE GIRLS

reference: I HAVE NOTHING TO WEAR REPLIED THAT DEMURE PERSON

reference: IT HAS OCCUPIED MOTHER A LONG TIME TO FIND AT THE SHOPS THE EXACT SHADE FOR HER NEW BONNET

reference: AND THEE WON'T GO WHY SHOULD I

reference: IF I GO TO MEETING AT ALL I LIKE BEST TO SIT IN THE QUIET OLD HOUSE IN GERMANTOWN WHERE THE WINDOWS ARE ALL OPEN AND I CAN SEE THE TREES AND HEAR THE STIR OF THE LEAVES

reference: IT'S SUCH A CRUSH AT THE YEARLY MEETING AT ARCH STREET AND THEN THERE'S THE ROW OF SLEEK LOOKING YOUNG MEN WHO LINE THE CURBSTONE AND STARE AT US AS WE COME OUT

reference: HE DOESN'T SAY BUT IT'S ON THE FRONTIER AND ON THE MAP EVERYTHING BEYOND IT IS MARKED INDIANS AND DESERT AND LOOKS AS DESOLATE AS A WEDNESDAY MEETING HUMPH IT WAS TIME FOR HIM TO DO SOMETHING

reference: IS HE GOING TO START A DAILY NEWSPAPER AMONG THE KICK A POOS

transcript: [" father, these unjust to Philip, he's going into business."]
reference: FATHER THEE'S UNJUST TO PHILIP HE'S GOING INTO BUSINESS

reference: HE DOESN'T SAY EXACTLY WHAT IT IS SAID RUTH A LITTLE DUBIOUSLY BUT IT'S SOMETHING ABOUT LAND AND RAILROADS AND THEE KNOWS FATHER THAT FORTUNES ARE MADE NOBODY KNOWS EXACTLY HOW IN A NEW COUNTRY

reference: BUT PHILIP IS HONEST AND HE HAS TALENT ENOUGH IF HE WILL STOP SCRIBBLING TO MAKE HIS WAY

reference: WHAT A BOX WOMEN ARE PUT INTO MEASURED FOR IT AND PUT IN YOUNG IF WE GO ANYWHERE IT'S IN A BOX VEILED AND PINIONED AND SHUT IN BY DISABILITIES

transcript: [' Why should I rust and be stupid and sit in inaction because I am a girl?']
reference: WHY SHOULD I RUST AND BE STUPID AND SIT IN INACTION BECAUSE I AM A GIRL

transcript: [' And if I had a fortune, would thee want me to lead a useless life?']
reference: AND IF I HAD A FORTUNE WOULD THEE WANT ME TO LEAD A USELESS LIFE

reference: HAS THEE CONSULTED THY MOTHER ABOUT A CAREER I SUPPOSE IT IS A CAREER THEE WANTS

transcript: [' But that wise and placid woman understood the sweet rebel a great deal better than ruth understood herself.']
reference: BUT THAT WISE AND PLACID WOMAN UNDERSTOOD THE SWEET REBEL A GREAT DEAL BETTER THAN RUTH UNDERSTOOD HERSELF

transcript: [' ruth was glad to hear that philip had made a push into the world and she was sure that his talent and courage would make a way for him']
reference: RUTH WAS GLAD TO HEAR THAT PHILIP HAD MADE A PUSH INTO THE WORLD AND SHE WAS SURE THAT HIS TALENT AND COURAGE WOULD MAKE A WAY FOR HIM

reference: BUT THE MORE FORGETFULNESS HAD THEN PREVAILED THE MORE POWERFUL WAS THE FORCE OF REMEMBRANCE WHEN SHE AWOKE

transcript: [" Miss Milner's health is not good."]
reference: MISS MILNER'S HEALTH IS NOT GOOD

transcript: [' said missus horton a few minutes after']
reference: SAID MISSUS HORTON A FEW MINUTES AFTER

transcript: [' So there is to me added Sandford with a sarcastic sneer.']
reference: SO THERE IS TO ME ADDED SANDFORD WITH A SARCASTIC SNEER

transcript: [' And yet you must own her behaviour has warranted them has it not been in this particular incoherent and unaccountable?']
reference: AND YET YOU MUST OWN HER BEHAVIOUR HAS WARRANTED THEM HAS IT NOT BEEN IN THIS PARTICULAR INCOHERENT AND UNACCOUNTABLE

transcript: [' Not that I know of, not one more that I know of, he replied with astonishment at what she had insinuated and yet with a perfect assurance that she was in the wrong.']
reference: NOT THAT I KNOW OF NOT ONE MORE THAT I KNOW OF HE REPLIED WITH ASTONISHMENT AT WHAT SHE HAD INSINUATED AND YET WITH A PERFECT ASSURANCE THAT SHE WAS IN THE WRONG

transcript: [' perhaps I am mistaken answered she.']
reference: PERHAPS I AM MISTAKEN ANSWERED SHE

transcript: [' To ask any more questions of you I believe would be unfair.']
reference: TO ASK ANY MORE QUESTIONS OF YOU I BELIEVE WOULD BE UNFAIR

transcript: [' He seemed to wait for her reply, but as she made none, he proceeded.']
reference: HE SEEMED TO WAIT FOR HER REPLY BUT AS SHE MADE NONE HE PROCEEDED

transcript: [' Oh, my lord! cried miss woodley with a most forcible accent, you are the last person on earth she would pardon me for intrusting.']
reference: OH MY LORD CRIED MISS WOODLEY WITH A MOST FORCIBLE ACCENT YOU ARE THE LAST PERSON ON EARTH SHE WOULD PARDON ME FOR ENTRUSTING

reference: BUT IN SUCH A CASE MISS MILNER'S ELECTION OF A HUSBAND SHALL NOT DIRECT MINE

transcript: [' If she does not know how to estimate her own value, I do.']
reference: IF SHE DOES NOT KNOW HOW TO ESTIMATE HER OWN VALUE I DO

reference: IS SHE NOT AFRAID THAT I WILL THWART HER INCLINATIONS

reference: AGAIN HE SEARCHED HIS OWN THOUGHTS NOR INEFFECTUALLY AS BEFORE

reference: I WILL MAKE NO UNJUST USE OF WHAT I KNOW HE REPLIED WITH FIRMNESS I BELIEVE YOU MY LORD

reference: I HAVE NEVER YET HOWEVER BEEN VANQUISHED BY THEM AND EVEN UPON THIS OCCASION MY REASON SHALL COMBAT THEM TO THE LAST AND MY REASON SHALL FAIL ME BEFORE I DO WRONG

reference: YES DEAD THESE FOUR YEARS AN A GOOD JOB FOR HER TOO

transcript: [" Well, as I say, it's an awful queer world. They clap all the burglars in jail and the murderers and the wife beaters. I allers thought a gentle reproof would be enough punishment for a wife beater cause he prob'ly has a lot of provocation that nobody knows. And the fire bugs can't think of the right name something like cendiaries. And the breakers of the peace and what not, and yet the law has nothing to say to a man like Han Lord."]
reference: WELL AS I SAY IT'S AN AWFUL QUEER WORLD THEY CLAP ALL THE BURGLARS INTO JAIL AND THE MURDERERS AND THE WIFE BEATERS I'VE ALLERS THOUGHT A GENTLE REPROOF WOULD BE ENOUGH PUNISHMENT FOR A WIFE BEATER CAUSE HE PROBABLY HAS A LOT O PROVOCATION THAT NOBODY KNOWS AND THE FIREBUGS CAN'T THINK O THE RIGHT NAME SOMETHING LIKE CENDENARIES AN THE BREAKERS O THE PEACE AN WHAT NOT AN YET THE LAW HAS NOTHIN TO SAY TO A MAN LIKE HEN LORD

reference: GRANDFATHER WAS ALEXANDER CAREY L L D DOCTOR OF LAWS THAT IS

reference: MISTER POPHAM LAID DOWN HIS BRUSH

reference: DONE HE AIN'T DONE A THING HE'D OUGHTER SENCE HE WAS BORN

reference: HE KEEPS THE THOU SHALT NOT COMMANDMENTS FIRST RATE HEN LORD DOES

reference: HE GIVE UP HIS POSITION AND SHUT THE FAMILY UP IN THAT TOMB OF A HOUSE SO T HE COULD STUDY HIS BOOKS

reference: MISTER POPHAM EXAGGERATED NOTHING BUT ON THE CONTRARY LEFT MUCH UNSAID IN HIS NARRATIVE OF THE FAMILY AT THE HOUSE OF LORDS

reference: HENRY LORD WITH THE DEGREE OF PH D TO HIS CREDIT HAD BEEN PROFESSOR OF ZOOLOGY AT A NEW ENGLAND COLLEGE BUT HAD RESIGNED HIS POST IN ORDER TO WRITE A SERIES OF SCIENTIFIC TEXT BOOKS

reference: ALWAYS IRRITABLE COLD INDIFFERENT HE HAD GROWN RAPIDLY MORE SO AS YEARS WENT ON

reference: WHATEVER APPEALED TO HER SENSE OF BEAUTY WAS STRAIGHTWAY TRANSFERRED TO PAPER OR CANVAS

reference: SHE IS WILD TO KNOW HOW TO DO THINGS

reference: SHE MAKES EFFORT AFTER EFFORT TREMBLING WITH EAGERNESS AND WHEN SHE FAILS TO REPRODUCE WHAT SHE SEES SHE WORKS HERSELF INTO A FRENZY OF GRIEF AND DISAPPOINTMENT

reference: WHEN SHE COULD NOT MAKE A RABBIT OR A BIRD LOOK REAL ON PAPER SHE SEARCHED IN HER FATHER'S BOOKS FOR PICTURES OF ITS BONES

reference: HE WOULDN'T SEARCH SO DON'T WORRY REPLIED CYRIL QUIETLY AND THE TWO LOOKED AT EACH OTHER AND KNEW THAT IT WAS SO

reference: OLIVE'S MOURNFUL BLACK EYES MET NANCY'S SPARKLING BROWN ONES

reference: SHE'S WONDERFUL MORE WONDERFUL THAN ANYBODY WE'VE EVER SEEN ANYWHERE AND SHE DRAWS BETTER THAN THE TEACHER IN CHARLESTOWN

reference: SHE'S OLDER THAN I AM BUT SO TINY AND SAD AND SHY THAT SHE SEEMS LIKE A CHILD

reference: NATTY HARMON TRIED THE KITCHEN PUMP SECRETLY SEVERAL TIMES DURING THE EVENING FOR THE WATER HAD TO RUN UP HILL ALL THE WAY FROM THE WELL TO THE KITCHEN SINK AND HE BELIEVED THIS TO BE A CONTINUAL MIRACLE THAT MIGHT GIVE OUT AT ANY MOMENT

transcript: [' To night there was no need of extra heat and there were great ceremonies to be observed in lighting the fires on the hearthstones']
reference: TO NIGHT THERE WAS NO NEED OF EXTRA HEAT AND THERE WERE GREAT CEREMONIES TO BE OBSERVED IN LIGHTING THE FIRES ON THE HEARTHSTONES

reference: THEY BEGAN WITH THE ONE IN THE FAMILY SITTING ROOM COLONEL WHEELER RALPH THURSTON MISTER AND MISSUS BILL HARMON WITH NATTY AND RUFUS MISTER AND MISSUS POPHAM WITH DIGBY AND LALLIE JOY ALL STANDING IN ADMIRING GROUPS AND THRILLING WITH DELIGHT AT THE ORDER OF EVENTS

transcript: [' Kathleen waved the torch to and fro as she recited some beautiful lines written for some such purpose as that which called them together to night']
reference: KATHLEEN WAVED THE TORCH TO AND FRO AS SHE RECITED SOME BEAUTIFUL LINES WRITTEN FOR SOME SUCH PURPOSE AS THAT WHICH CALLED THEM TOGETHER TO NIGHT

transcript: [' Burn fire burn flicker flicker flame.']
reference: BURN FIRE BURN FLICKER FLICKER FLAME

reference: NEXT CAME OLIVE'S TURN TO HELP IN THE CEREMONIES

reference: RALPH THURSTON HAD FOUND A LINE OF LATIN FOR THEM IN HIS BELOVED HORACE TIBI SPLENDET FOCUS FOR YOU THE HEARTH FIRE SHINES

reference: OLIVE HAS ANOTHER LOVELY GIFT FOR THE YELLOW HOUSE SAID MOTHER CAREY RISING AND TO CARRY OUT THE NEXT PART OF THE PROGRAMME WE SHALL HAVE TO GO IN PROCESSION UPSTAIRS TO MY BEDROOM

reference: EXCLAIMED BILL HARMON TO HIS WIFE AS THEY WENT THROUGH THE LIGHTED HALL

reference: AIN'T THEY THE GREATEST

reference: MOTHER CAREY POURED COFFEE NANCY CHOCOLATE AND THE OTHERS HELPED SERVE THE SANDWICHES AND CAKE DOUGHNUTS AND TARTS

reference: AT THAT MOMENT THE GENTLEMAN ENTERED BEARING A HUGE OBJECT CONCEALED BY A PIECE OF GREEN FELT

reference: THINKS I TO MYSELF I NEVER SEEN ANYTHING OSH POPHAM COULDN'T MEND IF HE TOOK TIME ENOUGH AND GLUE ENOUGH SO I CARRIED THIS LITTLE FELLER HOME IN A BUSHEL BASKET ONE NIGHT LAST MONTH AN I'VE SPENT ELEVEN EVENIN'S PUTTIN HIM TOGETHER

reference: MISSUS HARMON THOUGHT HE SANG TOO MUCH AND TOLD HER HUSBAND PRIVATELY THAT IF HE WAS A CANARY BIRD SHE SHOULD WANT TO KEEP A TABLE COVER OVER HIS HEAD MOST OF THE TIME BUT HE WAS IMMENSELY POPULAR WITH THE REST OF HIS AUDIENCE

reference: THE FACE OF THE MAHOGANY SHONE WITH DELIGHT AND WHY NOT WHEN IT WAS DOING EVERYTHING ALMOST EVERYTHING WITHIN THE SCOPE OF A PIANO AND YET THE FAMILY HAD ENJOYED WEEKS OF GOOD NOURISHING MEALS ON WHAT HAD BEEN SAVED BY ITS EXERTIONS

reference: WE SHUT OUR EYES THE FLOWERS BLOOM ON WE MURMUR BUT THE CORN EARS FILL WE CHOOSE THE SHADOW BUT THE SUN THAT CASTS IT SHINES BEHIND US STILL

reference: LENGTH OF SERVICE FOURTEEN YEARS THREE MONTHS AND FIVE DAYS

reference: HE SEEMED BORN TO PLEASE WITHOUT BEING CONSCIOUS OF THE POWER HE POSSESSED

reference: ONCE IN ACTION HE WAS LEADING A DETACHMENT OF INFANTRY THROUGH AN INTRENCHMENT

reference: NO CATHEDRAL NOT EVEN BURGOS ITSELF COULD VIE WITH THE CHURCH AT MONTMARTRE

reference: BEN ZOOF'S MOST AMBITIOUS DESIRE WAS TO INDUCE THE CAPTAIN TO GO WITH HIM AND END HIS DAYS IN HIS MUCH LOVED HOME AND SO INCESSANTLY WERE SERVADAC'S EARS BESIEGED WITH DESCRIPTIONS OF THE UNPARALLELED BEAUTIES AND ADVANTAGES OF THIS EIGHTEENTH ARRONDISSEMENT OF PARIS THAT HE COULD SCARCELY HEAR THE NAME OF MONTMARTRE WITHOUT A CONSCIOUS THRILL OF AVERSION

reference: WHEN A PRIVATE IN THE EIGHTH CAVALRY HE HAD BEEN ON THE POINT OF QUITTING THE ARMY AT TWENTY EIGHT YEARS OF AGE BUT UNEXPECTEDLY HE HAD BEEN APPOINTED ORDERLY TO CAPTAIN SERVADAC

reference: THE BOND OF UNION THUS EFFECTED COULD NEVER BE SEVERED AND ALTHOUGH BEN ZOOF'S ACHIEVEMENTS HAD FAIRLY EARNED HIM THE RIGHT OF RETIREMENT HE FIRMLY DECLINED ALL HONORS OR ANY PENSION THAT MIGHT PART HIM FROM HIS SUPERIOR OFFICER

reference: UNLIKE HIS MASTER HE MADE NO PRETENSION TO ANY GIFT OF POETIC POWER BUT HIS INEXHAUSTIBLE MEMORY MADE HIM A LIVING ENCYCLOPAEDIA AND FOR HIS STOCK OF ANECDOTES AND TROOPER'S TALES HE WAS MATCHLESS

reference: FAST AS HIS LEGS COULD CARRY HIM SERVADAC HAD MADE HIS WAY TO THE TOP OF THE CLIFF

transcript: [" It was quite true that a vessel was in sight hardly more than six miles from the shore but owing to the increase in the earth's convexity and the consequent limitation of the range of vision the rigging of the topmasts alone was visible above the water"]
reference: IT WAS QUITE TRUE THAT A VESSEL WAS IN SIGHT HARDLY MORE THAN SIX MILES FROM THE SHORE BUT OWING TO THE INCREASE IN THE EARTH'S CONVEXITY AND THE CONSEQUENT LIMITATION OF THE RANGE OF VISION THE RIGGING OF THE TOPMASTS ALONE WAS VISIBLE ABOVE THE WATER

reference: EXCLAIMED SERVADAC KEEPING HIS EYE UNMOVED AT HIS TELESCOPE

reference: SHE IS UNDER SAIL BUT SHE IS COUNT TIMASCHEFF'S YACHT HE WAS RIGHT

reference: IF THE COUNT WERE ON BOARD A STRANGE FATALITY WAS BRINGING HIM TO THE PRESENCE OF HIS RIVAL

reference: HE RECKONED THEREFORE NOT ONLY UPON ASCERTAINING THE EXTENT OF THE LATE CATASTROPHE BUT UPON LEARNING ITS CAUSE

reference: THE WIND BEING ADVERSE THE DOBRYNA DID NOT MAKE VERY RAPID PROGRESS BUT AS THE WEATHER IN SPITE OF A FEW CLOUDS REMAINED CALM AND THE SEA WAS QUITE SMOOTH SHE WAS ENABLED TO HOLD A STEADY COURSE

reference: SERVADAC TOOK IT FOR GRANTED THAT THE DOBRYNA WAS ENDEAVORING TO PUT IN

reference: A NARROW CHANNEL FORMED A PASSAGE THROUGH THE RIDGE OF ROCKS THAT PROTECTED IT FROM THE OPEN SEA AND WHICH EVEN IN THE ROUGHEST WEATHER WOULD ENSURE THE CALMNESS OF ITS WATERS

reference: SLIGHTLY CHANGING HER COURSE SHE FIRST STRUCK HER MAINSAIL AND IN ORDER TO FACILITATE THE MOVEMENTS OF HER HELMSMAN SOON CARRIED NOTHING BUT HER TWO TOPSAILS BRIGANTINE AND JIB

reference: CAPTAIN SERVADAC HASTENED TOWARDS HIM

transcript: [' I left you on a continent and here I have the honor of finding you on an island.']
reference: I LEFT YOU ON A CONTINENT AND HERE I HAVE THE HONOR OF FINDING YOU ON AN ISLAND

transcript: [' never mind now interposed the captain we will talk of that by and by']
reference: NEVER MIND NOW INTERPOSED THE CAPTAIN WE WILL TALK OF THAT BY AND BY

reference: NOTHING MORE THAN YOU KNOW YOURSELF

reference: ARE YOU CERTAIN THAT THIS IS THE MEDITERRANEAN

reference: FOR SOME MOMENTS HE SEEMED PERFECTLY STUPEFIED THEN RECOVERING HIMSELF HE BEGAN TO OVERWHELM THE COUNT WITH A TORRENT OF QUESTIONS

reference: TO ALL THESE INQUIRIES THE COUNT RESPONDED IN THE AFFIRMATIVE

reference: SOME MYSTERIOUS FORCE SEEMED TO HAVE BROUGHT ABOUT A CONVULSION OF THE ELEMENTS

reference: YOU WILL TAKE ME ON BOARD COUNT WILL YOU NOT

reference: MY YACHT IS AT YOUR SERVICE SIR EVEN SHOULD YOU REQUIRE TO MAKE A TOUR ROUND THE WORLD

reference: THE COUNT SHOOK HIS HEAD

reference: BEFORE STARTING IT WAS INDISPENSABLE THAT THE ENGINE OF THE DOBRYNA SHOULD BE REPAIRED TO SAIL UNDER CANVAS ONLY WOULD IN CONTRARY WINDS AND ROUGH SEAS BE BOTH TEDIOUS AND DIFFICULT

reference: IT WAS ON THE LAST DAY OF JANUARY THAT THE REPAIRS OF THE SCHOONER WERE COMPLETED

reference: A SLIGHT DIMINUTION IN THE EXCESSIVELY HIGH TEMPERATURE WHICH HAD PREVAILED FOR THE LAST FEW WEEKS WAS THE ONLY APPARENT CHANGE IN THE GENERAL ORDER OF THINGS BUT WHETHER THIS WAS TO BE ATTRIBUTED TO ANY ALTERATION IN THE EARTH'S ORBIT WAS A QUESTION WHICH WOULD STILL REQUIRE SEVERAL DAYS TO DECIDE

reference: DOUBTS NOW AROSE AND SOME DISCUSSION FOLLOWED WHETHER OR NOT IT WAS DESIRABLE FOR BEN ZOOF TO ACCOMPANY HIS MASTER

transcript: [' Her sea going qualities were excellent and would have amply sufficed for a circumnavigation of the globe.']
reference: HER SEA GOING QUALITIES WERE EXCELLENT AND WOULD HAVE AMPLY SUFFICED FOR A CIRCUMNAVIGATION OF THE GLOBE

transcript: [' After an apprenticeship on a merchant ship he had entered the imperial navy and had already reached the rank of lieutenant when the count appointed him to the charge of his own private yacht in which he was accustomed to spend by far the greater part of his time throughout the winter generally cruising in the mediterranean whilst in the summer he visited more northern waters']
reference: AFTER AN APPRENTICESHIP ON A MERCHANT SHIP HE HAD ENTERED THE IMPERIAL NAVY AND HAD ALREADY REACHED THE RANK OF LIEUTENANT WHEN THE COUNT APPOINTED HIM TO THE CHARGE OF HIS OWN PRIVATE YACHT IN WHICH HE WAS ACCUSTOMED TO SPEND BY FAR THE GREATER PART OF HIS TIME THROUGHOUT THE WINTER GENERALLY CRUISING IN THE MEDITERRANEAN WHILST IN THE SUMMER HE VISITED MORE NORTHERN WATERS

transcript: [' The late astounding events however had rendered procope manifestly uneasy and not the less so from his consciousness that the count secretly partook of his own anxiety.']
reference: THE LATE ASTOUNDING EVENTS HOWEVER HAD RENDERED PROCOPE MANIFESTLY UNEASY AND NOT THE LESS SO FROM HIS CONSCIOUSNESS THAT THE COUNT SECRETLY PARTOOK OF HIS OWN ANXIETY

transcript: [' Steam up and canvas spread the schooner started eastwards.']
reference: STEAM UP AND CANVAS SPREAD THE SCHOONER STARTED EASTWARDS

transcript: [" Although only a moderate breeze was blowing the sea was rough, a circumstance to be accounted for only by the diminution in the force of the earth's attraction rendering the liquid particles so buoyant that by the mere effect of oscillation they were carried to a height that was quite unprecedented."]
reference: ALTHOUGH ONLY A MODERATE BREEZE WAS BLOWING THE SEA WAS ROUGH A CIRCUMSTANCE TO BE ACCOUNTED FOR ONLY BY THE DIMINUTION IN THE FORCE OF THE EARTH'S ATTRACTION RENDERING THE LIQUID PARTICLES SO BUOYANT THAT BY THE MERE EFFECT OF OSCILLATION THEY WERE CARRIED TO A HEIGHT THAT WAS QUITE UNPRECEDENTED

transcript: [' For a few miles she followed the line hitherto presumably occupied by the coast of algeria but no land appeared to the south.']
reference: FOR A FEW MILES SHE FOLLOWED THE LINE HITHERTO PRESUMABLY OCCUPIED BY THE COAST OF ALGERIA BUT NO LAND APPEARED TO THE SOUTH

reference: THE LOG AND THE COMPASS THEREFORE WERE ABLE TO BE CALLED UPON TO DO THE WORK OF THE SEXTANT WHICH HAD BECOME UTTERLY USELESS

transcript: [" There's no fear of that, sir."]
reference: THERE IS NO FEAR OF THAT SIR

transcript: [' The earth has undoubtedly entered upon a new orbit, but she is not incurring any probable risk of being precipitated on to the sun']
reference: THE EARTH HAS UNDOUBTEDLY ENTERED UPON A NEW ORBIT BUT SHE IS NOT INCURRING ANY PROBABLE RISK OF BEING PRECIPITATED ONTO THE SUN

transcript: [' And what demonstration do you offer asked servadac eagerly that it will not happen?']
reference: AND WHAT DEMONSTRATION DO YOU OFFER ASKED SERVADAC EAGERLY THAT IT WILL NOT HAPPEN

transcript: [' Ocean reigned supreme.']
reference: OCEAN REIGNED SUPREME

transcript: [' All the images of his past life floated upon his memory his thoughts sped away to his native France only to return again to wonder whether the depths of ocean would reveal any traces of the algerian metropolis.']
reference: ALL THE IMAGES OF HIS PAST LIFE FLOATED UPON HIS MEMORY HIS THOUGHTS SPED AWAY TO HIS NATIVE FRANCE ONLY TO RETURN AGAIN TO WONDER WHETHER THE DEPTHS OF OCEAN WOULD REVEAL ANY TRACES OF THE ALGERIAN METROPOLIS

transcript: [' Is it not impossible he murmured aloud that any city should disappear so completely?']
reference: IS IT NOT IMPOSSIBLE HE MURMURED ALOUD THAT ANY CITY SHOULD DISAPPEAR SO COMPLETELY

transcript: [' Would not the loftiest eminences of the city at least be visible?']
reference: WOULD NOT THE LOFTIEST EMINENCES OF THE CITY AT LEAST BE VISIBLE

transcript: [' Another circumstance was most remarkable.']
reference: ANOTHER CIRCUMSTANCE WAS MOST REMARKABLE

transcript: [' To the surprise of all and especially of Lieutenant Procope the line indicated a bottom at a nearly uniform depth of from four to five fathoms and although the sounding was persevered with continuously for more than two hours over a considerable area the differences of level were insignificant not corresponding in any degree to what would be expected over the site of a city that had been terraced like the seats of an amphitheatre']
reference: TO THE SURPRISE OF ALL AND ESPECIALLY OF LIEUTENANT PROCOPE THE LINE INDICATED A BOTTOM AT A NEARLY UNIFORM DEPTH OF FROM FOUR TO FIVE FATHOMS AND ALTHOUGH THE SOUNDING WAS PERSEVERED WITH CONTINUOUSLY FOR MORE THAN TWO HOURS OVER A CONSIDERABLE AREA THE DIFFERENCES OF LEVEL WERE INSIGNIFICANT NOT CORRESPONDING IN ANY DEGREE TO WHAT WOULD BE EXPECTED OVER THE SITE OF A CITY THAT HAD BEEN TERRACED LIKE THE SEATS OF AN AMPHITHEATER

transcript: [' You must see, lieutenant, I should think, that we are not so near the coast of Algeria as you imagined.']
reference: YOU MUST SEE LIEUTENANT I SHOULD THINK THAT WE ARE NOT SO NEAR THE COAST OF ALGERIA AS YOU IMAGINED

transcript: [' After pondering a while he said if we were farther away I should expect to find a depth of two or three hundred fathoms instead of five fathoms five fathoms']
reference: AFTER PONDERING AWHILE HE SAID IF WE WERE FARTHER AWAY I SHOULD EXPECT TO FIND A DEPTH OF TWO OR THREE HUNDRED FATHOMS INSTEAD OF FIVE FATHOMS FIVE FATHOMS

transcript: [' its depth remained invariable still four or at most five fathoms and although its bottom was assiduously dredged it was only to prove it barren of marine production of any type.']
reference: ITS DEPTH REMAINED INVARIABLE STILL FOUR OR AT MOST FIVE FATHOMS AND ALTHOUGH ITS BOTTOM WAS ASSIDUOUSLY DREDGED IT WAS ONLY TO PROVE IT BARREN OF MARINE PRODUCTION OF ANY TYPE

transcript: [' Nothing was to be done but to put about and return in disappointment toward the north.']
reference: NOTHING WAS TO BE DONE BUT TO PUT ABOUT AND RETURN IN DISAPPOINTMENT TOWARDS THE NORTH

transcript: [' At another time Harold asked,']
reference: AT ANOTHER TIME HARALD ASKED

transcript: [" What is your country, Olaf? Have you always been a thrall? The thrall's eyes flashed."]
reference: WHAT IS YOUR COUNTRY OLAF HAVE YOU ALWAYS BEEN A THRALL THE THRALL'S EYES FLASHED

transcript: [' Two hundred warriors feasted in his hall and followed him to battle.']
reference: TWO HUNDRED WARRIORS FEASTED IN HIS HALL AND FOLLOWED HIM TO BATTLE

transcript: [' The rest of you off a viking. He had three ships.']
reference: THE REST OF YOU OFF A VIKING HE HAD THREE SHIPS

transcript: [' These he gave to three of my brothers.']
reference: THESE HE GAVE TO THREE OF MY BROTHERS

transcript: [' But I stayed that spring and built me a boat.']
reference: BUT I STAYED THAT SPRING AND BUILT ME A BOAT

reference: I MADE HER FOR ONLY TWENTY OARS BECAUSE I THOUGHT FEW MEN WOULD FOLLOW ME FOR I WAS YOUNG FIFTEEN YEARS OLD

reference: AT THE PROW I CARVED THE HEAD WITH OPEN MOUTH AND FORKED TONGUE THRUST OUT

transcript: [' I painted the eyes red for anger.']
reference: I PAINTED THE EYES RED FOR ANGER

transcript: [' There stand so I said and glare and hiss at my foes.']
reference: THERE STAND SO I SAID AND GLARE AND HISS AT MY FOES

transcript: [' In the stern I carved the tail up almost as high as the head.']
reference: IN THE STERN I CURVED THE TAIL UP ALMOST AS HIGH AS THE HEAD

transcript: [' There she sat on the rollers, as fair a ship as I ever saw.']
reference: THERE SHE SAT ON THE ROLLERS AS FAIR A SHIP AS I EVER SAW

transcript: [' Then I will get me a farm and will winter in that land now who will follow me?']
reference: THEN I WILL GET ME A FARM AND WILL WINTER IN THAT LAND NOW WHO WILL FOLLOW ME

transcript: [' He is but a boy the man said.']
reference: HE IS BUT A BOY THE MEN SAID

transcript: [' thirty men one after another raised their horns and said,']
reference: THIRTY MEN ONE AFTER ANOTHER RAISED THEIR HORNS AND SAID

transcript: [' As our boat flashed down the rollers into the water, I made this song and sang it.']
reference: AS OUR BOAT FLASHED DOWN THE ROLLERS INTO THE WATER I MADE THIS SONG AND SANG IT

transcript: [' So we harried the coast of norway.']
reference: SO WE HARRIED THE COAST OF NORWAY

transcript: [" We ate at many men's tables uninvited."]
reference: WE ATE AT MANY MEN'S TABLES UNINVITED

transcript: [" My dragon's belly is never full' and on board went the gold."]
reference: MY DRAGON'S BELLY IS NEVER FULL AND ON BOARD WENT THE GOLD

transcript: [' Oh, it is better to live on the sea and let other men raise your crops and cook your meals.']
reference: OH IT IS BETTER TO LIVE ON THE SEA AND LET OTHER MEN RAISE YOUR CROPS AND COOK YOUR MEALS

transcript: [' A house smells of smoke, a ship smells of frolic.']
reference: A HOUSE SMELLS OF SMOKE A SHIP SMELLS OF FROLIC

transcript: [' Up and down the water we went to get much wealth and much frolic.']
reference: UP AND DOWN THE WATER WE WENT TO GET MUCH WEALTH AND MUCH FROLIC

transcript: [' What of the farm, Olaf? Not yet, I answered Viking is better for summer.']
reference: WHAT OF THE FARM OLAF NOT YET I ANSWERED VIKING IS BETTER FOR SUMMER

transcript: [' It was so dark that I could see nothing but a few sparks on the hearth.']
reference: IT WAS SO DARK THAT I COULD SEE NOTHING BUT A FEW SPARKS ON THE HEARTH

transcript: [' I stood with my back to the wall, for I wanted no sword reaching out of the dark for me.']
reference: I STOOD WITH MY BACK TO THE WALL FOR I WANTED NO SWORD REACHING OUT OF THE DARK FOR ME

transcript: [' Come, come, I called when no one obeyed a fire.']
reference: COME COME I CALLED WHEN NO ONE OBEYED A FIRE

transcript: [' My men laughed. Yes, a stingy hose.']
reference: MY MEN LAUGHED YES A STINGY HOST

transcript: [' He acts as though he had not expected us.']
reference: HE ACTS AS THOUGH HE HAD NOT EXPECTED US

transcript: [' On a bench in a far corner were a dozen people huddled together.']
reference: ON A BENCH IN A FAR CORNER WERE A DOZEN PEOPLE HUDDLED TOGETHER

transcript: [' Bring in the table we are hungry.']
reference: BRING IN THE TABLE WE ARE HUNGRY

transcript: [' The thralls were bringing in a great pot of meat.']
reference: THE THRALLS WERE BRINGING IN A GREAT POT OF MEAT

transcript: [' They set up a crane over the fire and hung the pot upon it and we sat and watched it boil while we joked at last the supper began']
reference: THEY SET UP A CRANE OVER THE FIRE AND HUNG THE POT UPON IT AND WE SAT AND WATCHED IT BOIL WHILE WE JOKED AT LAST THE SUPPER BEGAN

reference: THE FARMER SAT GLOOMILY ON THE BENCH AND WOULD NOT EAT AND YOU CANNOT WONDER FOR HE SAW US PUTTING POTFULS OF HIS GOOD BEEF AND BASKET LOADS OF BREAD INTO OUR BIG MOUTHS

transcript: [' You would not eat with us. You cannot say no to half of my ale. I drink this to your health.']
reference: YOU WOULD NOT EAT WITH US YOU CANNOT SAY NO TO HALF OF MY ALE I DRINK THIS TO YOUR HEALTH

transcript: [' Then I drank half of the hornful and set the rest across the fire to the farmer. He took it and smiled, saying,']
reference: THEN I DRANK HALF OF THE HORNFUL AND SENT THE REST ACROSS THE FIRE TO THE FARMER HE TOOK IT AND SMILED SAYING

transcript: [' Did you ever have such a lordly guest before? I went on.']
reference: DID YOU EVER HAVE SUCH A LORDLY GUEST BEFORE I WENT ON

transcript: [' So I will give out this law that my men shall never leave you alone.']
reference: SO I WILL GIVE OUT THIS LAW THAT MY MEN SHALL NEVER LEAVE YOU ALONE

transcript: [' Hawkin there shall be your constant companion, friend farmer.']
reference: HAKON THERE SHALL BE YOUR CONSTANT COMPANION FRIEND FARMER

transcript: [' He shall not leave you day or night whether you are working or playing or sleeping.']
reference: HE SHALL NOT LEAVE YOU DAY OR NIGHT WHETHER YOU ARE WORKING OR PLAYING OR SLEEPING

transcript: [' I named nine others and said,']
reference: I NAMED NINE OTHERS AND SAID

transcript: [' And these shall follow your thralls in the same way.']
reference: AND THESE SHALL FOLLOW YOUR THRALLS IN THE SAME WAY

transcript: [' So I set guards over every one in that house.']
reference: SO I SET GUARDS OVER EVERY ONE IN THAT HOUSE

transcript: [' So no tales got out to the neighbors besides it was a lonely place and by good luck no one came that way.']
reference: SO NO TALES GOT OUT TO THE NEIGHBORS BESIDES IT WAS A LONELY PLACE AND BY GOOD LUCK NO ONE CAME THAT WAY

transcript: [' Their eyes danced Big Torleif stood up and stretched himself.']
reference: THEIR EYES DANCED BIG THORLEIF STOOD UP AND STRETCHED HIMSELF

transcript: [" I'm stiff with long sitting he said. I itch for a fight. I turned to the farmer."]
reference: I AM STIFF WITH LONG SITTING HE SAID I ITCH FOR A FIGHT I TURNED TO THE FARMER

transcript: [' This is our last feast with you, I said.']
reference: THIS IS OUR LAST FEAST WITH YOU I SAID

transcript: [' By the beard of odin, I cried, you have taken our joke like a man.']
reference: BY THE BEARD OF ODIN I CRIED YOU HAVE TAKEN OUR JOKE LIKE A MAN

transcript: [' My men pounded the table with their fists.']
reference: MY MEN POUNDED THE TABLE WITH THEIR FISTS

transcript: [' By the hammer of Thor shouted Grim, there is no stingy coward.']
reference: BY THE HAMMER OF THOR SHOUTED GRIM HERE IS NO STINGY COWARD

transcript: [" Here friend, take it, and he thrust it into the farmer's hand."]
reference: HERE FRIEND TAKE IT AND HE THRUST IT INTO THE FARMER'S HAND

transcript: [" May you drink heart's ease from it for many years."]
reference: MAY YOU DRINK HEART'S EASE FROM IT FOR MANY YEARS

transcript: [' And with it I leave you a name, sif the friendly. I shall hope to drink with you sometime in Valhalla.']
reference: AND WITH IT I LEAVE YOU A NAME SIF THE FRIENDLY I SHALL HOPE TO DRINK WITH YOU SOMETIME IN VALHALLA

reference: HERE IS A RING FOR SIF THE FRIENDLY AND HERE IS A BRACELET A SWORD WOULD NOT BE ASHAMED TO HANG AT YOUR SIDE

reference: I TOOK FIVE GREAT BRACELETS OF GOLD FROM OUR TREASURE CHEST AND GAVE THEM TO HIM

reference: THAT IS THE BEST WAY TO DECIDE FOR THE SPEAR WILL ALWAYS POINT SOMEWHERE AND ONE THING IS AS GOOD AS ANOTHER

transcript: [" That time it pointed us into your father's ships."]
reference: THAT TIME IT POINTED US INTO YOUR FATHER'S SHIPS

reference: HERE THEY SAID IS A RASCAL WHO HAS BEEN HARRYING OUR COASTS

transcript: [' We sunk his ship and men, but him we brought to you.']
reference: WE SUNK HIS SHIP AND MEN BUT HIM WE BROUGHT TO YOU

transcript: [' A robber viking? said the king, and he scowled at me.']
reference: A ROBBER VIKING SAID THE KING AND SCOWLED AT ME

transcript: [' Yes, and with all your fingers it took you a year to catch me. The king frowned more angrily.']
reference: YES AND WITH ALL YOUR FINGERS IT TOOK YOU A YEAR TO CATCH ME THE KING FROWNED MORE ANGRILY

transcript: [' Take him out, Torkel, and let him taste your sword.']
reference: TAKE HIM OUT THORKEL AND LET HIM TASTE YOUR SWORD

reference: YOUR MOTHER THE QUEEN WAS STANDING BY

transcript: [' now she put her hand on his arm and smiled and said']
reference: NOW SHE PUT HER HAND ON HIS ARM AND SMILED AND SAID

transcript: [' And would he not be a good gift for our baby?']
reference: AND WOULD HE NOT BE A GOOD GIFT FOR OUR BABY

transcript: [' Your father thought a moment, then looked at your mother and smiled.']
reference: YOUR FATHER THOUGHT A MOMENT THEN LOOKED AT YOUR MOTHER AND SMILED

transcript: [' soft heart he said gently to her then to Torquil well let him go, Torquil.']
reference: SOFT HEART HE SAID GENTLY TO HER THEN TO THORKEL WELL LET HIM GO THORKEL

transcript: [' Then he turned to me again frowning.']
reference: THEN HE TURNED TO ME AGAIN FROWNING

transcript: [' But young sharp tongue, now that we have caught you, we will put you into a trap that you cannot get out of.']
reference: BUT YOUNG SHARP TONGUE NOW THAT WE HAVE CAUGHT YOU WE WILL PUT YOU INTO A TRAP THAT YOU CANNOT GET OUT OF

transcript: [" So I lived and now I'm your tooth thrall. Well, it is the luck of war."]
reference: SO I LIVED AND NOW AM YOUR TOOTH THRALL WELL IT IS THE LUCK OF WAR

transcript: [' It was one of the masterly and charming stories of Dumas the elder.']
reference: IT WAS ONE OF THE MASTERLY AND CHARMING STORIES OF DUMAS THE ELDER

transcript: [' In five minutes I was in a new world and my melancholy room was full of the liveliest french company.']
reference: IN FIVE MINUTES I WAS IN A NEW WORLD AND MY MELANCHOLY ROOM WAS FULL OF THE LIVELIEST FRENCH COMPANY

transcript: [' The sound of an imperative and uncompromising bell recalled me in due time to the regions of reality.']
reference: THE SOUND OF AN IMPERATIVE AND UNCOMPROMISING BELL RECALLED ME IN DUE TIME TO THE REGIONS OF REALITY

transcript: [' Ambrose met me at the bottom of the stairs and showed me the way to the supper room.']
reference: AMBROSE MET ME AT THE BOTTOM OF THE STAIRS AND SHOWED ME THE WAY TO THE SUPPER ROOM

transcript: [' She signed to me with a ghostly solemnity to take the vacant place on the left of her father.']
reference: SHE SIGNED TO ME WITH A GHOSTLY SOLEMNITY TO TAKE THE VACANT PLACE ON THE LEFT OF HER FATHER

transcript: [' The door opened again while I was still studying the two brothers without, I honestly confess, being very favorably impressed by either of them.']
reference: THE DOOR OPENED AGAIN WHILE I WAS STILL STUDYING THE TWO BROTHERS WITHOUT I HONESTLY CONFESS BEING VERY FAVORABLY IMPRESSED BY EITHER OF THEM

reference: A NEW MEMBER OF THE FAMILY CIRCLE WHO INSTANTLY ATTRACTED MY ATTENTION ENTERED THE ROOM

transcript: [' A little cracked, that in the popular phrase was my impression of the stranger who now made his appearance in the supper room.']
reference: A LITTLE CRACKED THAT IN THE POPULAR PHRASE WAS MY IMPRESSION OF THE STRANGER WHO NOW MADE HIS APPEARANCE IN THE SUPPER ROOM

transcript: [' mister Meadowcroft the elder having not spoken one word thus far himself introduced the newcomer to me with a side glance at his sons which had something like defiance in it a glance which as I was sorry to notice was returned with the defiance on their side by the two young men']
reference: MISTER MEADOWCROFT THE ELDER HAVING NOT SPOKEN ONE WORD THUS FAR HIMSELF INTRODUCED THE NEWCOMER TO ME WITH A SIDE GLANCE AT HIS SONS WHICH HAD SOMETHING LIKE DEFIANCE IN IT A GLANCE WHICH AS I WAS SORRY TO NOTICE WAS RETURNED WITH THE DEFIANCE ON THEIR SIDE BY THE TWO YOUNG MEN

reference: PHILIP LEFRANK THIS IS MY OVERLOOKER MISTER JAGO SAID THE OLD MAN FORMALLY PRESENTING US

reference: HE IS NOT WELL HE HAS COME OVER THE OCEAN FOR REST AND CHANGE OF SCENE

transcript: [' mister Iago is an American, Philip.']
reference: MISTER JAGO IS AN AMERICAN PHILIP

transcript: [' Make acquaintance with miss Chiago sit together.']
reference: MAKE ACQUAINTANCE WITH MISTER JAGO SIT TOGETHER

reference: THEY POINTEDLY DREW BACK FROM JOHN JAGO AS HE APPROACHED THE EMPTY CHAIR NEXT TO ME AND MOVED ROUND TO THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF THE TABLE

reference: A PRETTY GIRL AND SO FAR AS I COULD JUDGE BY APPEARANCES A GOOD GIRL TOO DESCRIBING HER GENERALLY I MAY SAY THAT SHE HAD A SMALL HEAD WELL CARRIED AND WELL SET ON HER SHOULDERS BRIGHT GRAY EYES THAT LOOKED AT YOU HONESTLY AND MEANT WHAT THEY LOOKED A TRIM SLIGHT LITTLE FIGURE TOO SLIGHT FOR OUR ENGLISH NOTIONS OF BEAUTY A STRONG AMERICAN ACCENT AND A RARE THING IN AMERICA A PLEASANTLY TONED VOICE WHICH MADE THE ACCENT AGREEABLE TO ENGLISH EARS

transcript: [' Our first impressions of people are in nine cases out of ten the right impressions.']
reference: OUR FIRST IMPRESSIONS OF PEOPLE ARE IN NINE CASES OUT OF TEN THE RIGHT IMPRESSIONS

transcript: [' For once in a way I proved a true prophet.']
reference: FOR ONCE IN A WAY I PROVED A TRUE PROPHET

transcript: [' The only cheerful conversation was the conversation across the table between Naomi and me.']
reference: THE ONLY CHEERFUL CONVERSATION WAS THE CONVERSATION ACROSS THE TABLE BETWEEN NAOMI AND ME

transcript: [' He looked up at Naomi doubtingly from his plate and looked down again slowly with a frown.']
reference: HE LOOKED UP AT NAOMI DOUBTINGLY FROM HIS PLATE AND LOOKED DOWN AGAIN SLOWLY WITH A FROWN

transcript: [' When I addressed him, he answered constrainedly.']
reference: WHEN I ADDRESSED HIM HE ANSWERED CONSTRAINEDLY

transcript: [' A more dreary and more disunited family party I never sat at table with.']
reference: A MORE DREARY AND MORE DISUNITED FAMILY PARTY I NEVER SAT AT THE TABLE WITH

transcript: [' Envy hatred malice and uncharitableness are never so essentially detestable to my mind as when they are animated by the sense of propriety and work under the surface. But for my interest in Naomi and my other interest in the little love looks which I now and then surprised passing between her and ambrose, I should never have sat through that supper.']
reference: ENVY HATRED MALICE AND UNCHARITABLENESS ARE NEVER SO ESSENTIALLY DETESTABLE TO MY MIND AS WHEN THEY ARE ANIMATED BY A SENSE OF PROPRIETY AND WORK UNDER THE SURFACE BUT FOR MY INTEREST IN NAOMI AND MY OTHER INTEREST IN THE LITTLE LOVE LOOKS WHICH I NOW AND THEN SURPRISED PASSING BETWEEN HER AND AMBROSE I SHOULD NEVER HAVE SAT THROUGH THAT SUPPER

transcript: [" I wish you good night. She laid her bony hands on the back of mister meadowcroft's invalid chair, cut him short in his farewell salutation to me, and wheeled him out to his bed as if she were wheeling him out to his grave."]
reference: I WISH YOU GOOD NIGHT SHE LAID HER BONY HANDS ON THE BACK OF MISTER MEADOWCROFT'S INVALID CHAIR CUT HIM SHORT IN HIS FAREWELL SALUTATION TO ME AND WHEELED HIM OUT TO HIS BED AS IF SHE WERE WHEELING HIM OUT TO HIS GRAVE

transcript: [' You were quite right to say no, Ambrose began. Never smoke with John Iago. His cigars will poison you.']
reference: YOU WERE QUITE RIGHT TO SAY NO AMBROSE BEGAN NEVER SMOKE WITH JOHN JAGO HIS CIGARS WILL POISON YOU

transcript: [' Naomi shook her forefinger reproachfully at them as if the two sturdy young farmers had been two children.']
reference: NAOMI SHOOK HER FOREFINGER REPROACHFULLY AT THEM AS IF THE TWO STURDY YOUNG FARMERS HAD BEEN TWO CHILDREN

transcript: [' Silas slunk away without a word of protest ambrose stood his ground evidently bent on making his peace with naomi before he left her seeing that I was in the way I walked aside toward a glass door at the lower end of the room']
reference: SILAS SLUNK AWAY WITHOUT A WORD OF PROTEST AMBROSE STOOD HIS GROUND EVIDENTLY BENT ON MAKING HIS PEACE WITH NAOMI BEFORE HE LEFT HER SEEING THAT I WAS IN THE WAY I WALKED ASIDE TOWARD A GLASS DOOR AT THE LOWER END OF THE ROOM

transcript: [' It is manifest that man is now subject to much variability.']
reference: IT IS MANIFEST THAT MAN IS NOW SUBJECT TO MUCH VARIABILITY

transcript: [' So it is with the lower animals.']
reference: SO IT IS WITH THE LOWER ANIMALS

transcript: [' The variability of multiple parts.']
reference: THE VARIABILITY OF MULTIPLE PARTS

transcript: [' But this subject will be more properly discussed when we treat of the different races of mankind.']
reference: BUT THIS SUBJECT WILL BE MORE PROPERLY DISCUSSED WHEN WE TREAT OF THE DIFFERENT RACES OF MANKIND

transcript: [' Effects of the increased use and disuse of parts.']
reference: EFFECTS OF THE INCREASED USE AND DISUSE OF PARTS

transcript: [' Chapter seven on the races of man.']
reference: CHAPTER SEVEN ON THE RACES OF MAN

transcript: [' In determining whether two or more allied forms ought to be ranked as species or varieties naturalists are practically guided by the following considerations namely the amount of difference between them and whether such differences relate to few or many points of structure and whether they are of physiological importance but more especially whether they are constant']
reference: IN DETERMINING WHETHER TWO OR MORE ALLIED FORMS OUGHT TO BE RANKED AS SPECIES OR VARIETIES NATURALISTS ARE PRACTICALLY GUIDED BY THE FOLLOWING CONSIDERATIONS NAMELY THE AMOUNT OF DIFFERENCE BETWEEN THEM AND WHETHER SUCH DIFFERENCES RELATE TO FEW OR MANY POINTS OF STRUCTURE AND WHETHER THEY ARE OF PHYSIOLOGICAL IMPORTANCE BUT MORE ESPECIALLY WHETHER THEY ARE CONSTANT

transcript: [" Eleven o'clock had struck it was a fine clear night they were the only persons on the road and they sauntered leisurely along to avoid paying the price of fatigue for the recreation provided for the toledans in the valley or on the banks of their river"]
reference: ELEVEN O'CLOCK HAD STRUCK IT WAS A FINE CLEAR NIGHT THEY WERE THE ONLY PERSONS ON THE ROAD AND THEY SAUNTERED LEISURELY ALONG TO AVOID PAYING THE PRICE OF FATIGUE FOR THE RECREATION PROVIDED FOR THE TOLEDANS IN THEIR VALLEY OR ON THE BANKS OF THEIR RIVER

transcript: [' Secure as he thought in the careful administration of justice in that city and the character of its well disposed inhabitants, the good hidalgo was far from thinking that any disaster could befall his family.']
reference: SECURE AS HE THOUGHT IN THE CAREFUL ADMINISTRATION OF JUSTICE IN THAT CITY AND THE CHARACTER OF ITS WELL DISPOSED INHABITANTS THE GOOD HIDALGO WAS FAR FROM THINKING THAT ANY DISASTER COULD BEFAL HIS FAMILY

transcript: [' Rodolpho and his companions, with their faces muffled in their cloaks, stared rudely and insolently at the mother, the daughter, and the servant maid.']
reference: RODOLFO AND HIS COMPANIONS WITH THEIR FACES MUFFLED IN THEIR CLOAKS STARED RUDELY AND INSOLENTLY AT THE MOTHER THE DAUGHTER AND THE SERVANT MAID

transcript: [' In a moment he communicated his thoughts to his companions, and in the next moment they resolved to turn back and carry her off to please Rodolpho. For the rich who are open handed always find parricides ready to encourage their bad propensities. And thus to conceive this wicked design, to communicate it, approve it, resolve on ravishing Leocadia, and to carry that design into effect was the work of a moment.']
reference: IN A MOMENT HE COMMUNICATED HIS THOUGHTS TO HIS COMPANIONS AND IN THE NEXT MOMENT THEY RESOLVED TO TURN BACK AND CARRY HER OFF TO PLEASE RODOLFO FOR THE RICH WHO ARE OPEN HANDED ALWAYS FIND PARASITES READY TO ENCOURAGE THEIR BAD PROPENSITIES AND THUS TO CONCEIVE THIS WICKED DESIGN TO COMMUNICATE IT APPROVE IT RESOLVE ON RAVISHING LEOCADIA AND TO CARRY THAT DESIGN INTO EFFECT WAS THE WORK OF A MOMENT

transcript: [' They drew their swords hid their faces in the flaps of their cloaks turned back and soon came in front of the little party who had not yet done giving thanks to God for their escape from those audacious men.']
reference: THEY DREW THEIR SWORDS HID THEIR FACES IN THE FLAPS OF THEIR CLOAKS TURNED BACK AND SOON CAME IN FRONT OF THE LITTLE PARTY WHO HAD NOT YET DONE GIVING THANKS TO GOD FOR THEIR ESCAPE FROM THOSE AUDACIOUS MEN

reference: FINALLY THE ONE PARTY WENT OFF EXULTING AND THE OTHER WAS LEFT IN DESOLATION AND WOE

transcript: [" Rodolpho arrived at his own house without any impediment and Leocadia's parents reached theirs heart broken and despairing."]
